Afghanistan DHS 2015. A literacy test was administered to men and women aged 15-49 whose highest level of educational attendance was primary schooling or lower. The test consisted of an enumerator administered assessment on the ability of the respondent to read a short and simple sentence. Those who could partially or fully read the sentence are considered literate. Respondents above the educational attainment threshold for whom the test was not administered were imputed as literate. Literacy status was imputed via a statistical model for older age groups not covered by the assessment.

UIS based on HIES - Living Standards Survey: ILO Harmonized Microdata, https://ilostat.ilo.org/. Respondents aged 3 and above were asked to self-assess their literacy through two separate questions: first, whether they could read a short text, and second, whether they could write a short text, in each of the following languages: Kirundi, French, English, Swahili, or another language, responding yes or no to each question.

Literacy definition:
A person is literate who can, with understanding, both read and write a short simple statement on his or her everyday life.
Source: Population Census

UIS based on HIES - Monitoring Survey of the Modular and Integrated Survey of Household Living Conditions: ILO Harmonized Microdata, https://ilostat.ilo.org/. Respondents aged 3 years and above were classified as literate or not based on their self-assessed ability to read and write a short text in French and/or national languages. Those who reported being able to read and write in French, in national languages, or in both were coded as literate, while those who reported being unable to read or write in either language were coded as not literate.

Rwanda DHS 2019-20. A literacy test was administered to women aged 15-49 and men 15-59 whose highest level of educational attendance was primary schooling or lower. The test consisted of an enumerator administered assessment on the ability of the respondent to read a short and simple sentence. Literacy status was imputed via a statistical model for older age groups not covered by the assessment.

UIS based on HS - Integrated Household Survey: ILO Harmonized Microdata, https://ilostat.ilo.org/. A literacy test was administered to respondents aged 10 years and above. By the interviewer, respondents were asked to self-assess and demonstrate their literacy and numeracy skills in English and any Sierra Leonean language. They were asked to read and write sentences in English and in a local language (Mende, Temne, Krio, Limba, or other) using flash cards or the manual, and to perform written mathematics calculations using a provided problem card. Responses were recorded by the interviewer as correct/incorrect or by the specific language read/written.

Tunisia National Survey on Household Budget, Consumption and Standard of Living (EBCNV) 2021-22. Respondents 3 years of age who declared not being at school at the time of the interview were asked to declare the languages in which could read and write well (Arabic, French, English or any other language). Respondents who declared having attended at least lower secondary education were assumed to be literate (UIS).

Tunisia National Population and Employment Survey (ENPE) 2023 fourth quarter. Respondents of all ages who declared not attending school at the time of the survey were asked to declare whether they could read and write in any other language by responding yes/no.

Tanzania DHS 2022. A literacy test was administered to men and women aged 15-49 whose highest level of educational attendance was lower secondary schooling or lower. The test consisted of an enumerator administered assessment on the ability of the respondent to read a short and simple sentence. Those who could partially or fully read the sentence are considered literate. Respondents above the educational attainment threshold for whom the test was not administered were imputed as literate. Literacy status was imputed via a statistical model for older age groups not covered by the assessment.

Uganda DHS 2016. A literacy test was administered to women aged 15-49 and men 15-54 whose highest level of educational attendance was tertiary schooling or lower. The test consisted of an enumerator administered assessment on the ability of the respondent to read a short and simple sentence. Those who could partially or fully read the sentence are considered literate. Respondents above the educational attainment threshold for whom the test was not administered were imputed as literate. Literacy status was imputed via a statistical model for older age groups not covered by the assessment.
